texto: Natural science is founded on minute critical views of the general order of events taking place upon our globe, corrected, enlarged, or exalted by experiments, in which the agents concerned are placed under new circumstances, and their diversified properties separately examined. The body of natural science, then, consists of facts; is analogy,—the relation of resemblance of facts by which its different parts are connected, arranged, and employed, either for popular use, or for new speculative improvements.

texto: Nothing is so dangerous to the progress of the human mind than to assume that our views of science are ultimate, that there are no mysteries in nature, that our triumphs are complete and that there are no new worlds to conquer.

texto: In the present state of our knowledge, it would be useless to attempt to speculate on the remote cause of the electrical energy... its relation to chemical affinity is, however, sufficiently evident. May it not be identical with it, and an essential property of matter?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Humphry Davy que provoca temas ligados a sabedoria. Ao questionar “In the present state of our knowledge, it would be useless to attempt to speculate on the remote …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre sabedoria, energy, present, com foco em present e matter.

texto: Language is not only the vehicle of thought, it is a great and efficient instrument in thinking.
